brief_justification: Arrives at exactly ~9.1%, matching the ground truth, with fully valid Bayesian derivation; explores base rate neglect, natural frequencies, clinical implications, and 900-fold prior update — multiple analytical levels; explicitly names the intuitive trap (base rate neglect) upfront but does not do mid-analysis course-correction or name further biases; independently verifies via both algebraic Bayes and natural frequency counting, two distinct derivation paths converging on same answer; acknowledges the result is 'far from diagnostic certainty' and explains why, but does not identify which assumptions (e.g., patient risk subgroup) would shift the conclusion; addresses the wrong intuitive answer and explains why it fails, but does not enumerate other competing approaches or priors; every variable, formula, intermediate step, and natural frequency calculation is shown — fully reproducible; explicitly avoids letting the prior persist unchanged and names base rate neglect as the key failure pattern; avoids all suppressed failure modes including ignoring population frequency, single hypothesis lock, and belief state rigidity; follows the topology's PRIOR→evidence→LR→Bayes→posterior→output path but skips the M1 meta-check gate and does not make branching structure explicit
